title: Abstract Signal: Trump explique pourquoi diatement

news.title: Trump explique pourquoi il n’a pas immédiatement été évacué du gala des correspondants après les tirs : “C’est un peu de ma faute”

elements.scenePurpose: Establishes the full-frame painted ochre temple surface and prevents the work from reading as a digital stage.
elements.expectedRead: aged mineral pigment on matte canvas

elements.scenePurpose: Makes the opposing red-orange altar geometry the main body of the painting, replacing the failed rectangular panel read.
elements.expectedRead: authoritative Af Klint-like temple triangles with gold-painted edges

elements.scenePurpose: Declares the main structural law: a ceremonial vertical line that is cleanly interrupted above center.
elements.expectedRead: thin gold law with a deliberate missing section

elements.scenePurpose: Turns the admission of partial fault into a small, sober, stable mark below the break.
elements.expectedRead: blue responsibility seal with restrained gold contours

elements.scenePurpose: Adds controlled pressure around the interrupted axis so the order feels disturbed without becoming literal violence.

representation.label: Interrupted Temple Axis
representation.description: The rebuilt canonical composition: a frontal ochre temple painting organized by a clean broken gold axis, with opposing red-orange triangles carrying the structural weight and a small blue seal below the interruption.
representation.scenePrompt: Full-frame matte Af Klint temple painting with red-orange opposing altar triangles, thin broken gold vertical axis, small blue accountability seal below the break, and sparse reddish fracture writing around the gap.
representation.colorMood: ochre mineral warmth with disciplined red, orange, gold, and one sober blue focal point
representation.spatialIntent: flat frontal painted surface with shallow layer separation: ground behind, triangle altar midground, axis and seal foreground, fracture marks accenting the break

imageAuthorship.structuralLaw: One vertical law organizes the whole canvas and is deliberately interrupted above center.
imageAuthorship.edgeBehavior: clean symbolic edges softened by absorbed pigment and slight hand-painted irregularity
imageAuthorship.surfaceBehavior: matte mineral wash with subtle grain, not glossy emission
imageAuthorship.depthLogic: background ground, midground altar triangles, foreground gold law and blue seal, accent linework clustered near the break
imageAuthorship.motionLogic: nearly still, with only slow pigment breathing and a barely perceptible shimmer along the gold line

technicalTranslation.realizationStrategy: Layer deterministic full-frame shader planes in orthographic/frontal alignment. Use custom triangle, axis, seal, ground, and fracture shaders so the composition remains a painted surface rather than a reusable house-styl
technicalTranslation.whyThisApproach: The critique requires replacing the governing composition, not tuning an existing reticle-like module. Custom shaders keep exact control over the gap, triangle authority, seal scale, and linework visibility.
notes: Keep all planes coplanar or very shallow in z to avoid screen-stage drift.
Use alpha layering, not bloom, for gold authority.
Do not add outer seal rings beyond two contours.

mustShow: clear empty gap in the vertical gold axis above center
large opposing red-orange triangles as the main altar geometry
small blue disc with one or two gold contour rings below the break
5-8 visible thin reddish-brown fracture marks near the gap
matte ochre painted ground with mineral variation
reviewFocus: axis interruption must govern the image
triangles must read as primary altar structure
seal must remain small and non-reticle
fracture marks must be visible but controlled

surfaceQualities: painted surface behavior matters more than object literalism

rationale.critiqueResponse: This directly demotes the reticle and makes the interrupted vertical axis plus triangle altarpiece the main read.
rationale.newsTranslation: The delayed evacuation becomes an interrupted path; the quoted partial fault becomes the small blue seal held below the break.

artisticIntent.statement: A frontal temple painting about a delayed exit and a partial admission of fault, translated through Hilma af Klint as a broken ceremonial order rather than a political illustration.
artisticIntent.motif: interrupted vertical law held inside opposing altar triangles, with a small blue-gol
artisticIntent.emotionalArc: quiet order, sudden structural hesitation, then a restrained admission held in a small sober focal mark
artisticIntent.compositionLogic: The image is governed by one thin vertical gold axis that visibly stops and resumes above center. Large red-orange opposing triangles form the altar body around it, while sparse automatic-writing fracture marks gather near the interruption. The seal remains sm
artisticIntent.imagePrompt: A full-frame matte Hilma af Klint-inspired temple painting on warm ochre mineral ground: two large opposing red and orange altar triangles with clean gold edges, a narrow vertical gold line broken by a clear empty gap above center, a small sober blue disc below the gap with one or two thin gold contour rings, and spars

techniques.name: absorbed ochre pigment field
techniques.purpose: keeps the work as a painted frontal canvas rather than a digital backdrop

techniques.name: large opposing altar triangles
techniques.purpose: replaces the prior boxy panel with the main Af Klint-like temple structure

techniques.name: broken ceremonial vertical law
techniques.purpose: makes the interruption the governing structure of the image

techniques.name: small blue accountability seal
techniques.purpose: compresses responsibility into a small sober focal anchor without forming a target

techniques.name: automatic-writing fracture marks
techniques.purpose: introduces pressure around the break without literal violence

selection.whyHeadline: Selected as top-ranked Belgian headline (score 69 = frequency 69 + priority 0 + recency 0).

intent.statement: Trump explique pourquoi il n’a pas immédiatement été évacué du gala des correspondants après les tirs : “C’est un peu de ma faute” translated through Hilma af Klint's visual language as a non-literal image.
intent.coreImpulse: In Hilma af Klint: Paintings for the Temple (paintings by Hilma af Klint) Af Klint concluded Paintings for the Temple with a triptych she called Altarpieces, featuring geometric shapes, bold primary colors, and gold l... Hilma af Klint was a Swedish artist and mystic whose paintings are considered to be among the first major abstract works in Western art history. After studying at the Royal Academy of Art in Stockholm, Hilma af Klint embarked on a major suite of paintings in 1904-06. Hilma af Klint did not ascribe to any of the established avant-garde movements of her time.
artistTranslationLogic: Hilma af Klint's formal logic should dominate before any spectacle or scenic excess.

visualGravity.dominantStructure: Build the image around one readable structural law.
visualGravity.scaleBehavior: Keep the space bounded and compositionally intentional.

materialLogic.handling: Hilma af Klint's surface should feel made, not generic.

tensionEngine.escalationMode: Let tension accumulate through structure, not narration.
tensionEngine.releaseValve: Allow only a narrow zone of release.

antiDriftRules: Do not collapse into biography summary, symbolic cliché, or generic procedural spectacle.

After studying at the Royal Academy of Art in Stockholm, Hilma af Klint embarked on a major suite of paintings in 1904-06.
